label: Salesforce
description: Arcade tools designed for LLMs to interact with Salesforce

tools.name: ConvertLead
tools.description:
Converts a lead into a Contact (and optionally Account + Opportunity).

This is the canonical lead-to-deal transition in Salesforce. The lead record
is marked as converted and new Contact/Account/Opportunity records are created.

parameters.name: lead_id
parameters.description: The Salesforce ID of the lead to convert.

parameters.name: account_id
parameters.description: Existing Account ID to link the converted lead to. If omitted, Salesforce creates a new Account from the lead's Company.

parameters.name: opportunity_name
parameters.description: Name for the new opportunity created during conversion.

parameters.name: converted_status
parameters.description: The lead status to set upon conversion. Auto-detected if not provided.

parameters.name: skip_opportunity
parameters.description: If true, no opportunity is created during conversion. Defaults to False.

output.description: Conversion result with new contact, account, and opportunity IDs.

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

parameters.name: account_id
parameters.description: The ID of the account to create the contact for.

parameters.name: last_name
parameters.description: The last name of the contact.

parameters.name: first_name
parameters.description: The first name of the contact.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: The email of the contact.

parameters.name: phone
parameters.description: The phone number of the contact.

parameters.name: mobile_phone
parameters.description: The mobile phone number of the contact.

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: The title of the contact. E.g. 'CEO', 'Sales Director', 'CTO', etc.

parameters.name: department
parameters.description: The department of the contact. E.g. 'Marketing', 'Sales', 'IT', etc.

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: The description of the contact.

description.value: A key contact for new business opportunities.

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: CreateLead
tools.description:
Creates a new lead in Salesforce.

Use this when someone is a potential customer but isn't yet associated with
an existing account. For contacts under existing accounts, use create_contact.

parameters.name: last_name
parameters.description: The last name of the lead.

parameters.name: company
parameters.description: The company the lead works for.

parameters.name: first_name
parameters.description: The first name of the lead.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: The lead's email address.

parameters.name: phone
parameters.description: The lead's phone number.

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: The lead's job title.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Initial lead status. Must match the org's configured values. Defaults to the org's default status if not provided.

parameters.name: lead_source
parameters.description: How this lead originated. Must match the org's configured lead source values.

parameters.name: industry
parameters.description: The lead's industry.

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: Notes about the lead.

parameters.name: website
parameters.description: The lead's company website.

description.value: Interested in a product demo and pricing details. Follow up scheduled for next week.

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: CreateOpportunity
tools.description:
Creates a new opportunity (deal) in Salesforce.

Requires an account, name, stage, and close date. Validates stage, lead source,
and type against the org's configured picklist values.

parameters.name: account_id
parameters.description: The Salesforce ID of the Account to associate with.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: A descriptive name for the opportunity.

parameters.name: stage
parameters.description: The initial pipeline stage. Must match one of the org's configured stage names.

parameters.name: close_date
parameters.description: Expected close date in ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD).

parameters.name: amount
parameters.description: Deal amount in the org's default currency.

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: Free-text description or notes about the deal.

parameters.name: next_step
parameters.description: The immediate next action to advance this deal.

parameters.name: lead_source
parameters.description: How this opportunity originated. Must match the org's configured lead source values.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: The opportunity type. Must match the org's configured opportunity type values.

description.value: Full site redesign including responsive layout, CMS migration, and onboarding.

next_step.value: Send formal proposal and statement of work

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: CreateTask
tools.description:
Creates a task (follow-up, reminder, to-do) in Salesforce.

Use this for future actions like reminders, follow-ups, or to-do items.
For logging a completed phone call, use LogACall instead.

parameters.name: subject
parameters.description: Short description of the task.

parameters.name: due_date
parameters.description: When the task is due, in ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD).

parameters.name: priority
parameters.description: Task priority. Allowed values: High, Normal, Low.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Initial task status. Valid values are org-specific. Defaults to the org's default.

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: Detailed notes or context for the task.

parameters.name: account_or_opportunity_id
parameters.description: Salesforce ID of the Account or Opportunity this task is related to.

parameters.name: contact_or_lead_id
parameters.description: Salesforce ID of the Contact or Lead this task is about.

parameters.name: owner_user_id
parameters.description: Salesforce User ID to assign this task to. Defaults to the current user.

subject.value: Follow up on Q2 partnership proposal

description.value: Call the client to review the proposed terms, address questions, and schedule next steps. Reference proposal ID PR-2026-045.

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: GetAccountDataById
tools.description:
Gets the account with related info: contacts, leads, notes, calls, opportunities, tasks,
emails, and events (up to 10 items of each type).

An account is an organization (such as a customer, supplier, or partner, though more commonly
a customer). In some Salesforce account setups, an account can also represent a person.

parameters.name: account_id
parameters.description: The ID of the account to get data for.

output.description: The account with related info: contacts, leads, notes, calls, opportunities, tasks, emails, and events (up to 10 items of each type)

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: GetAccountDataByKeywords
tools.description:
Searches for accounts in Salesforce and returns them with related info: contacts, leads,
notes, calls, opportunities, tasks, emails, and events (up to 10 items of each type).

An account is an organization (such as a customer, supplier, or partner, though more commonly
a customer). In some Salesforce account setups, an account can also represent a person.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: The query to search for accounts. MUST be longer than one character. It will match the keywords against all account fields, such as name, website, phone, address, etc. E.g. 'Acme'

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of accounts to return. Defaults to 10. Maximum allowed is 10.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number to return. Defaults to 1 (first page of results).

output.description: The accounts matching the query with related info: contacts, leads, notes, calls, opportunities, tasks, emails, and events (up to 10 items of each type)

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: GetOpportunityById
tools.description:
Gets a single opportunity with enriched related data: contact roles, line items,
open tasks, and recent notes.

parameters.name: opportunity_id
parameters.description: The Salesforce ID of the opportunity.

output.description: The opportunity with enriched related data.

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: ListMyTasks
tools.description:
Lists tasks assigned to you in Salesforce with optional filters.

Excludes call logs and email activities — only shows actionable tasks.
Use overdue_only=true to see just tasks past their due date.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by task status. Values are org-specific.

parameters.name: due_date_from
parameters.description: Only return tasks due on or after this date (YYYY-MM-DD).

parameters.name: due_date_to
parameters.description: Only return tasks due on or before this date (YYYY-MM-DD).

parameters.name: overdue_only
parameters.description: If true, only return tasks that are past due and not yet completed. Defaults to False.

parameters.name: account_or_opportunity_id
parameters.description: Only return tasks linked to this Account or Opportunity.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum results to return (1-50). Defaults to 20.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number for pagination. Defaults to 1 (first page).

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: LogACall
tools.description:
Logs a completed phone call as an activity in Salesforce.

Use this to record calls that already happened. For future follow-up actions,
use CreateTask instead.

parameters.name: subject
parameters.description: Short description of the call.

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: Call notes, summary, or key takeaways.

parameters.name: duration_minutes
parameters.description: How long the call lasted, in minutes.

parameters.name: account_or_opportunity_id
parameters.description: Salesforce ID of the Account or Opportunity this call was about.

parameters.name: contact_or_lead_id
parameters.description: Salesforce ID of the Contact or Lead who was on the call.

parameters.name: call_result
parameters.description: The outcome of the call.

description.value: Customer provided feedback on integration requirements; agreed to review technical docs and schedule a follow-up demo next week.

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: SearchContacts
tools.description:
Searches for contacts in Salesforce with optional filters.

At least one filter (query, account_id, or title) must be provided.
Use this to find people at specific accounts or with specific roles.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Keyword search against contact name, email, title, and account name.

parameters.name: account_id
parameters.description: Only return contacts belonging to this Account.

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: Filter by job title (partial match).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum results to return (1-50). Defaults to 20.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number for pagination. Defaults to 1 (first page).

output.description: Paginated list of contacts matching the search criteria.

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: SearchLeads
tools.description:
Searches for leads in Salesforce with optional filters.

Always excludes already-converted leads. Use owner='me' (default) for your leads,
or owner='all' for all leads in the org.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Keyword search against lead name, company, title, and email.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by lead status. Must match one of the org's configured status values.

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: Filter by owner. Defaults to the current user. Pass 'all' to search across all owners.

parameters.name: lead_source
parameters.description: Filter by lead source. Must match the org's configured lead source values.

parameters.name: created_after
parameters.description: Only return leads created on or after this date (YYYY-MM-DD).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum results to return (1-50). Defaults to 20.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number for pagination. Defaults to 1 (first page).

output.description: Paginated list of leads matching the search criteria.

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: SearchOpportunities
tools.description:
Searches for opportunities (deals) in Salesforce with optional filters.

Returns a paginated list of opportunities with key fields like stage, amount,
close date, and associated account. Use owner='me' (default) to see your deals,
or owner='all' for the whole pipeline.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Keyword search against opportunity name.

parameters.name: stage
parameters.description: Filter by pipeline stage. Must match one of the org's configured stage names.

parameters.name: close_date_from
parameters.description: Only return opportunities closing on or after this date. ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD).

parameters.name: close_date_to
parameters.description: Only return opportunities closing on or before this date. ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD).

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: Filter by owner. Defaults to the current user. Pass 'all' to search across all owners.

parameters.name: min_amount
parameters.description: Minimum deal amount filter.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum results to return (1-50). Defaults to 20.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number for pagination. Defaults to 1 (first page).

output.description: Paginated list of opportunities matching the search criteria.

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: UpdateLead
tools.description:
Updates fields on an existing lead. Only provided fields are changed.

If the lead has already been converted, returns an error with the converted
record IDs so the agent can redirect to the correct contact/account.

parameters.name: lead_id
parameters.description: The Salesforce ID of the lead to update.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: New lead status. Must match the org's configured values.

parameters.name: first_name
parameters.description: Updated first name.

parameters.name: last_name
parameters.description: Updated last name.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Updated email address.

parameters.name: phone
parameters.description: Updated phone number.

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: Updated job title.

parameters.name: company
parameters.description: Updated company name.

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: Updated notes.

parameters.name: lead_source
parameters.description: Updated lead source. Must match the org's configured lead source values.

parameters.name: rating
parameters.description: Updated lead rating. Allowed values: Hot, Warm, Cold.

description.value: Followed up via email; requested a product demo for Q2 implementation.

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: UpdateOpportunity
tools.description:
Updates fields on an existing opportunity. Only provided fields are changed.

Returns a success indicator and any warnings (e.g., amount ignored due to line items).

parameters.name: opportunity_id
parameters.description: The Salesforce ID of the opportunity to update.

parameters.name: stage
parameters.description: New pipeline stage. Must match one of the org's configured stage names.

parameters.name: close_date
parameters.description: New expected close date (YYYY-MM-DD).

parameters.name: amount
parameters.description: Updated deal amount. May be ignored if the opportunity has line items.

parameters.name: next_step
parameters.description: Updated next action to advance the deal.

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: Updated description or notes.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Updated opportunity name.

parameters.name: probability
parameters.description: Override win probability (0-100).

parameters.name: forecast_category
parameters.description: Override forecast category.

description.value: Customer requested updated terms; awaiting legal approval.

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: UpdateTask
tools.description:
Updates fields on an existing task. Only provided fields are changed.

Use status='Completed' to mark a task as done.

parameters.name: task_id
parameters.description: The Salesforce ID of the task to update.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: New task status. Valid values are org-specific.

parameters.name: due_date
parameters.description: New due date (YYYY-MM-DD).

parameters.name: priority
parameters.description: New priority. Allowed values: High, Normal, Low.

parameters.name: subject
parameters.description: Updated task subject.

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: Updated description.

description.value: Left voicemail; will follow up via email to schedule a demo.

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: WhoAmI
tools.description:
Get information about the currently authenticated Salesforce user.

This is typically the first tool called to establish user context.
Returns the user's profile details and organization information.

output.description: Current user profile and Salesforce organization information.

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

summary:
Arcade Salesforce toolkit lets LLMs interact with Salesforce to read and modify CRM data, automate lead-to-deal workflows, and surface user and account context for agent operations.

**Capabilities**

- Unified CRM read/write: query and update accounts, contacts, leads, opportunities, tasks, and notes.
- Workflow orchestration: convert leads, create opportunities, assign tasks, and log activities.
- Contextual searches and enrichment: search by keywords/filters and retrieve related records and recent activity.
- User/organization context: obtain authenticated user and org info for permissions and personalization.

**OAuth**

Provider: salesforce

Scopes: read_account, read_contact, read_lead, read_note, read_opportunity, read_task, read_user, write_contact, write_lead, write_opportunity, write_task

**Secrets**

- Types: api_key and unknown. Examples: SALESFORCE_ORG_SUBDOMAIN (org subdomain string), SALESFORCE_MAX_CONCURRENT_REQUESTS (rate limit integer). API keys may be used for auxiliary integrations; OAuth remains the primary auth.
